observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a request for authority to write-off the dormant receivable accounts amounting to ₱6.56 million following the procedures in Sections 8.2 and 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management:

Continue to pursue improved practices in programming, prioritizing and implementing programs and projects to maximize the utilization of the Local Development Fund in attaining desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community;

Observe proper timing in the implementation of infrastructure projects to give ample time for the implementing office in the prompt accomplishment of the projects; and

Evaluate/assess the programs, projects and activities identified in the continuing appropriations to determine whether these are still feasible, otherwise, re-appropriate/reprogram the same to other priority development projects to be identified in the Annual Investment Program of the Municipality.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management maximize the utilization of its funds and ensure the implementation of PPAs designed to avoid, lessen or limit the potential adverse impact of disasters, such as those which pertains to disaster prevention/mitigation and disaster preparedness.

We recommended that Management include the list of projects, programs and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in the annual LDRRMFIP in compliance with Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular 2012-002.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

Maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for transfers of agency's unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year of transfer, receipts of NDRRMF by transferring agency, DRRMF from other LGUs by LGU and other sources by donor; and 

Disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management, through the GAD Focal Person, spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on the relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues and ensure that approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are gender responsive.  

Likewise, use the HGDG, PIMME and FIMME forms as tools in identifying PPAs attributed to GAD budget for efficiency in identifying the issues, analysis, and monitoring of its effectiveness.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II as Finding No. 25

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management collect from the supplier liquidated damages amounting to ₱77,847. Thereafter, exercise prudence in processing transactions pertaining to claims against the municipal fund and ensure the imposition of liquidated damages for late deliveries pursuant to the provisions in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to prevent suppliers from neglecting their responsibilities to comply with the conditions of the contract and to avoid delay in the implementation of projects/programs.
observations.reason: Liquidated damages could no longer be imposed due to the period that lapsed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant, through a Memorandum, copy furnished the Audit Team for monitoring purposes, to: (a) Prepare the aging schedules of the reconciled loans receivables under the Other Receivables accounts, that will aid in monitoring and collecting its receivables; and (b) Reconcile the subsidiary records of the Other Receivable accounts, with the general ledger balance, particularly with regard to the monthly amortization payment for the loans, which was not applied as deduction to the Other Receivables account and instead it was recorded as Other Payables, and to the interest income and make the necessary adjusting/correcting entries to properly record the loan payments and recognize the income earned.

We also reiterated the previous audit recommendation that the LGU send demand letters to the employee-borrowers with past due amortizations requesting immediate payment thereof and implement deduction from the salaries of delinquent borrowers should they fail to voluntarily settle their obligations.
observations.reason: Aging schedule is not yet available

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that the Accountable Officers be appropriately bonded in accordance with law.
We recommended that the Accountable Officer maintain physical copy of cashbook and observe its proper handling as prescribed under Section 26 of the NGAS.
We recommended that Management direct the Accountable Officer to obtain proper approval prior the release of petty cash fund and stop the practice of allowing frequent reimbursements.

We recommended that Management require the BAC, through BAC Secretariat, prepare the APP and PMR in accordance with the pertinent provisions in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipality comply with the posting requirements and submission to the GPPB to ensure transparency and integrity in the procurement process.

We recommended that Management require the personnel assigned in preparing the PO, IAR and RFQ to accomplish said forms completely and accurately not only to facilitate verification and monitoring but more significantly, to strengthen the internal control on procurement.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the prohibition of Chief accountant and personnel of accounting units to be a member of the BAC to ensure that the procurement and payment functions are segregated. Likewise, we recommend that Management ensure that the BAC regular members have undergone the basic training seminar on RA No. 9184 and its IRR to equip them with the procurement laws and processes.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly observe the prescribed procurement guidelines and procedures in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; and
Submit valid justification on the noted irregularities, otherwise the amount of ₱370,528.78 shall be disallowed in audit in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-003.
We recommended that Management submit valid justification for the award of contract in excess of the ABC. Otherwise, the said disbursement shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that Management, through the Accounting Office, ensure that the disbursement vouchers are adequately supported with the documentary requirements prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other pertinent regulations to avoid suspension and disallowances in audit.

We recommended that Management require the concerned personnel to submit the government contracts, purchase orders and the supporting documents within five days after perfection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to review the Subsidiary Ledger of Other Maintenance and Operating Expense accounts and reclassify the expenses into its specific general ledger accounts to ensure the fairness of information presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 in implementing projects funded by other agencies to ensure that funds are utilized and liquidated in a timely manner and that services and benefits due to the public are not hampered.

We recommended that Management request for write-off of receivable accounts which were dormant for more than 10 years after all the remedies have been exhausted, following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management:
Plan thoroughly the budget for the 20 per cent DF and pursue improved practices in programming, prioritizing and implementing programs and projects to maximize the implementation of development projects to ensure that optimum benefits are derived by the greater number of its constituents;
Meticulously and judiciously review and evaluate projects before its inclusion in the Local Development Fund and Annual Investment Plan and provide realistic timelines for its completion; and
Observe proper timing in the implementation of the infrastructure projects to give ample time for the implementing office in the prompt accomplishment of the projects.

We recommended that Management require proper coordination among the MDRRMO and Accounting Office regarding the LDRRMF and require the former to include the list of projects, programs and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in the annual LDRRMFIP in compliance with Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular 2012-002.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management maximize the utilization of DRRMF thru the timely implementation of projects and activities to reduce the vulnerabilities and risks to hazards and manage the consequences of disasters.

We recommended the Management require:
The MDRRMO to plan/identify PPAs to utilize the various cash donations according to its specific purpose and to address the needs of the intended beneficiaries; and
The Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis.
We recommended that Management maintain sufficient stockpile of relief goods and ensure that it is properly monitored and kept in a safe storage area.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for transfers of agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the special trust fund by year. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile their records to determine the unexpended balance from after five years to be reverted back to the unappropriated surplus.
Henceforth, we recommended that the transfer of unexpended/unobligated balance of QRF and DRRM- MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability -DRRM” in the trust fund books be recorded at the end of the year.

We recommended that Management require the concerned personnel to submit the lacking relief distribution sheets, logbook of days worked and other necessary documentation to support the validity and propriety of the disbursements. Henceforth, we recommended that Management, through the Accounting Office, ensure the adequacy of documentation to support the validity of the claims.
We recommended that Management continue to maximize the utilization of its SEF in order to deliver its services to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Solid Waste Management Board and all the Departments involve in planning and conceptualizing the Solid Waste Management Plan to give their full attention and cooperation in updating the Solid Waste Management Plan of the LGU to be able to comply with the requirement of the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management, through the GAD Focal Person, spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on the relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues and ensure that approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are gender responsive.
Likewise, use the HGDG, PIMME and FIMME forms as tools in identifying PPAs attributed to GAD budget for efficiency in identifying the issues, analysis, and monitoring of its effectiveness.

We recommended that Management revisit its GAD PPAs, providing more attention and study on how to maximize the utilization of the GAD in order to attain the objectives for which the funds were appropriated.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to remit in full the taxes withheld to the BIR within the time prescribed by BIR rules and regulations to avoid the payment of penalties incident to delayed remittance.

We recommended that Management:

Send demand letters to the DepEd employees with outstanding cash advances in the Municipality;

Stop the practice of granting cash advances to persons who are not employees of the Municipality considering that the granting thereof has no legal basis; and

Strictly adhere to existing guidelines on the allowable expenditures that are chargeable against the Special Education Fund.

We recommended that Management require:

The Municipal Treasurer to update the RPTAR /Taxpayer’s index card based on the Assessment Roll and furnish the Municipal Accounting Office the correct certified list of taxes due and collectible at the beginning of each year as basis in establishing the correct RPT/SET Receivables and the related Deferred RPT/Deferred SET Income accounts;

The Municipal Assessor to provide the Municipal Treasurer with the latest total assessment of the taxable properties as well as the taxes to be imposed before the 31st day of December of every year; and

The Municipal Accountant to review / reconcile the report before setting up the receivable account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Audit Team, for review and verification, the required documents and reports pertaining to fund transfers as of December 31, 2020.

We commended the Municipal Government for having fully complied with the reporting requirements prescribed in Item 4 of DBM LBC No. 125 for the BGCM funds received. Henceforth, continue to comply with the reporting requirements of funds transferred to the Municipality to enhance transparency and accountability in its transactions.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the perpetual inventory method is observed in the accounting of inventories to record supplies as assets using the inventory account and only recognize the portion of supplies inventories as expense upon consumption to reflect accurate balances of assets and expenses accounts in the financial statement.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to process claims with proper and complete supporting documents only.  In case of cash advances for payroll, two sets of payrolls must be prepared – one to be attached to the disbursement voucher for the cash advance and another as supporting document to the Liquidation Report of the cash advances, duly signed by the payees.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned personnel to prepare and submit the GAD Accomplishment Report in accordance with the prescribed period.

Likewise, the required accompanying reports to the GAD AR such as: (a) brief summary of the reported program or project; (b) copies of reported policy issuances; (c) results of HGDG tests; and (d) actions taken by the LGU on the COA audit findings and recommendations, shall be attached thereto.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (LCE): (a) Direct the GSO and the MAO to continue updating its Stock/Property Cards and Ledger Cards, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of property records and to immediately address any discrepancy found;   (b) Instruct the GSO and the MAO to trace property documents that could provide data such as dates of acquisition of the properties and its unit costs; and (c) In case the source documents could no longer be found, create an Appraisal Committee to conduct appraisal of properties without recorded costs/values but were included in the RPCPPE as of December 31, 2019 in order that the same may be taken up in the books of accounts.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the LCE assign personnel to work on securing the land titles of the 66 lots and instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds to cover its cost.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
Direct the GSO and the MAO to continue updating its Stock/Property Cards and Ledger Cards, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of property records and to immediately address any discrepancy found;
Instruct the GSO and the MAO to trace property documents that could provide data such as dates of acquisition of the properties and its unit costs; and
In case the source documents could no longer be found, create an Appraisal Committee to conduct appraisal of properties without recorded costs/values but were included in the RPCPPE as of December 31, 2019 in order that the same may be taken up in the books of accounts.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the LCE assign personnel to work on securing the land titles of the 66 lots and instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds to cover its cost.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer continue coordinating with the Municipal Accountant for the complete and immediate reconciliation of the individual balances of the Cash accounts in the GL and Cashbooks, respectively, and make the necessary adjustments thereon. Henceforth, in compliance with Section 189 of the GAAM, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant shall reconcile their books of accounts, at least quarterly, to avoid incurrence of accumulated unreconciled balances.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant include the details of all bank accounts in the Notes to Financial Statements and record all valid reconciling items in the books of accounts to fairly present the Cash accounts in the financial statements.
Furthermore, we reiterated our prior years’ recommendation for the LCE to initiate the creation of a team that shall assist the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant who both handled the GF Cashbook during the previous years to the locate necessary documents and reconcile the balances.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit Monthly TB and Quarterly FS with complete schedules and annexes within the prescribed timeframe in accordance with existing regulations;
Take appropriate immediate remedial measures, such as augmenting the Accountant’s Office of personnel from other Departments, especially during report preparation and/or allowing them to render overtime, subject to the availability of funds, in order to comply with the timely preparation and submission of these mandatory financial reports; and
Allow the Municipal Accountant to recommend qualified plantilla personnel of the Accounting Office to attend Trainings or Seminars relative to their works, to equip them with the required knowledge and skills in order to continue improving their performance.

We recommended that necessary updating and revisions to the LSWMP be given priority to expedite approval as required under R.A. No. 9003.
We also reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation to intensify the SWM information dissemination campaign and enforce all regulations to the extent of imposing penalties for non-compliance.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer to include among their priority activities the preparation of the POWs for SEF-funded infrastructure projects.
Further, we recommended that the LSB closely monitor the implementation of priority PPAs under the SEF to ensure that these are implemented and completed on time in accordance with the technical specifications.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the LSB strictly adhere to the formula presented in Form 1 of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 , s. 2017, in the proper computation of the SEF available for appropriation.
Further, we recommended that the LSB require the early submission of SIPs by the school heads, indicating therein priority needs of the schools based on DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 , s. 2017, in order that the same may be considered in the preparation of the SEF Budget.
Furthermore, we recommended that Management require complete. timely and accurate reports, data and information as basis in the preparation of budget estimates.

We recommended that the LSB promptly prepare and submit the required quarterly and annual reports to the oversight bodies, copy furnished the Auditor, and to strictly comply with the posting requirements regarding the utilization of the SEF.

We recommended that Management require the MTO and the MAO to reconcile the differences in the reported RPT-Basic and SET of their respective offices.  Henceforth, MTO to present the revenue/collection reports in accordance with Section 18 of the SRE Manual.
We also reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the MTO coordinate with the MAO on the use of the proper account titles and grouping of account title or sub-title to enhance efficiency and achieve uniformity in the recording and preparation of revenue reports and the annual budget.

We recommended that Management:
Require the GAD Focal Person to submit to the Audit Team a copy of the approved GAD GPB and AR for verification and audit;
To assign additional personnel who shall assist in the implementation, monitoring and reporting of the GAD PAPs.  These personnel may be sourced from the offices of the LGU with streamlined GAD PAPs in their operations; and
Ensure the implementation of GAD PAPs to achieve its mandate and purpose in compliance with laws, rules and regulations.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management maximize the utilization of its funds and ensure the implementation of PPAs designed to avoid, lessen or limit the potential adverse impact of disasters, such as those which pertains to disaster prevention/mitigation and disaster preparedness.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations that Management:
Strengthen local planning and facilitate the translation of the local development goals into implementable programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) in local development plans and investment programs.
Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) and Municipal Engineer to review and evaluate all the unimplemented targeted PPAs included in the current and prior years’ 20% LDF Plans and determine the causes of non-implementation to address the same. Henceforth require them to closely monitor and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development projects and programs under the 20% LDF that are envisioned to help in the realization of social-economic and environmental benefits.

We re-iterated paraphrased audit recommendations of the prior years that Management:

Direct the Treasurer and the Accountant to continue updating its Stock/Property Cards and Ledger Cards to facilitate the reconciliation of property records and to immediately address any discrepancy found; 

Direct all concerned personnel to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to Section 79 of P.D. No 1445, Sections 379 to 381 of R.A. No. 7160, COA Circular No. 89-296 and Section 125 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1; and

Direct the Municipal Assessor to work on securing the land titles of the 66 lots and require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds to cover its cost.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated paraphrased audit recommendations of the prior years that Management:
Direct the Treasurer and the Accountant to continue updating its Stock/Property Cards and Ledger Cards to facilitate the reconciliation of property records and to immediately address any discrepancy found;
Direct all concerned personnel to institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to Section 79 of P.D. No 1445, Sections 379 to 381 of R.A. No. 7160, COA Circular No. 89-296 and Section 125 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1; and
Direct the Municipal Assessor to work on securing the land titles of the 66 lots and require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds to cover its cost.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to:
Furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year and every year thereafter, that shall be the basis of the latter to set-up RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the accounting period to conform with Sections 19 and 20, Volume 1, of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs; and
Immediately post a notice of delinquency at the main hall and in publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each barangay in consonance with Section 254 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and such notice may also be posted in the LGU’s website.

We recommended that Management require:
The Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the immediate reconciliation of the individual balances of the Cash in Local Treasury and Cash in Bank accounts between the GL and the Cashbooks for all funds and make the necessary adjustments thereon. Henceforth, in compliance with Section 189 of the GAAM, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant shall reconcile their books of accounts at least quarterly to avoid incurrence of accumulated unreconciled balances; and
The Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Cash in Bank balances in the FS against balances in the BRS, particularly on the understatement amounting to ₱600 in GF account and the overstatement of ₱11,500 under TF account, and make the necessary adjustment.
We also recommended for the LCE to initiate the creation of a team that shall assist the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant who both handled the GFCashbook during the previous years to locate necessary documents,  trace causes/reasons for the discrepancies andto work and reconcile balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace and analyze the transactions, and reconcile the difference of ₱ 95,723 in the reciprocal accounts Due to/Due from Other Funds and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds as of December 31, 2018.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the timelines on the recording of accounts and submission of accounts and reports to the Auditor, within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month, as required by existing laws, rules and regulations.
We also recommended that Management look into the possibility of assigning additional manpower to the Accounting Office and allowing them to render overtime service, subject to availability of funds, in order to enable the Accountant to update the recording of transactions in the books, timely prepare and submit the accounts and financial reports within the reglementary period.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the timeline for the release to the barangays of their share from the RPT collection that is within five (5) days after the end of each quarter pursuant to Section 271(d) of R.A. No. 7160.

We reiterated prior year’s audit recommendation that Management maximize the utilization of its funds and ensure the implementation of programs, projects and activities designed to avoid, lessen or limit the potential adverse impact of disasters, such as those which pertains to disaster prevention/mitigation and disaster preparedness, shall be given utmost priority.

We recommended that:
The Treasurer’s Office coordinate with Accounting Unit on the use of account titles, grouping of account title or sub-title, particularly on Income Account, for efficiency of recording and preparation of collection reports in accordance with PPSAS:
Management indicate the projected amount on all expected revenues for the year, specifically in budget preparation in order to provide assurance in funding of plans and programs; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to revisit the collection accomplishment reports quarterly to come up with other possible measures in improving Municipality’s revenue collections as well as for efficient reporting.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) review the existing Local Revenue Code of Municipality of Tiwi and enact an ordinance to adjust the tax rates therein to comply with prescribed rates under the Local Government Code of 1991.
We recommended that Management facilitate the full implementation of projects funded by various agencies in order not to delay the benefits these could provide the intended beneficiaries.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation for Management to:
Pursue the immediate approval of the Ten- Year SWMP as required under R.A. No. 9003 to ensure its sustainability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international progress in the area of Solid Waste Management;
Persevere and continue exerting extra effort in pursuing the acquisition and installation of the waste disposal site and waste management facilities in accordance with Sections 41 and 42 of RA No. 9003; and
Intensify the SWM information dissemination campaign and enforce all regulations to the extent of imposing penalties for non-compliance.
We also recommended for the LGU to consider the establishment of clustered barangay MRF as prescribed in R.A. No. 9003 for the remaining barangays deprived of said facility.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to formulate/prepare the required three-year strategic program and the recommendation of the DepEd PSDS and SDS as well as the required SIPs and other information from DepEd RO, which supposedly the basis for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the 2018 SEF Annual Budget and also furnish the Audit Team of the same for reference and review purposes. Henceforth, the LSB strictly follow the mandated required processes and documents in planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF pursuant to the aforementioned JC. Otherwise, audit suspensions and disallowances may be issued by the Audit Team, if warranted.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to prepare and submit the required quarterly and annual reports to the oversight Department/Agency in the prescribed form and within the prescribed period and observe the posting of Utilization Report in the Agency/LGU website to facilitate effective monitoring of the allocation and utilization of the SEF thereby, promoting transparency and accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to submit to the LSB the complete and accurate data for the later to come up with the correct estimated appropriation for SEF in the ensuing years using the following formula:
Further, we recommended that concerned officers and employees closely monitor and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority projects and programs under the SEF that are envisioned to contribute to the financial support of the goals of education as provided by the Constitution.
Furthermore, for the Municipal Engineering Office to include in their priority activity/projects the preparation of Program of Work of SEF priority projects such as construction, repair and maintenance of school buildings and the likes.

We recommended that Management, through the GAD Focal Person, spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning,programming and policy formulation to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on the relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues and ensure that approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are gender responsive.  Likewise, use HGDG,PIMME and FIMME forms as tools in identifying programs and projects attributed to GAD budget for efficiency in identifying the issues, analysis, and monitoring its effectiveness.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officers/employees to look into the correctness of submitted GAD Plans and Budgets and Accomplishment Report and submit the correctedreports, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor for review and verification. Henceforth, Management shall ensure the reliability and integrity of informations/reports in order to effeciently plan, monitor andreport results.

We recommended that the Municipal Government strengthen local planning and facilitate the translation of the local development goals into implementable programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) in local development plans and investment programs.
Further, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer to review and evaluate all the unimplemented targeted PPAs included in the current and prior years’ 20% LDF Plans and determine the causes of non-implementation to address the same. Henceforth require them to closely monitor and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development projects and programs under the 20% LDF that are envisioned to help in the realization of social-economic and environmental benefits.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):

Direct the GSO and the Accountant to maintain a complete set of ledger cards and stock/property cards in accordance with the MNGAS rules and regulations. Exert extra effort in locating documents that can supply the needed information such as the historical/acquisition cost;

Instruct the Inventory Committee to reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records and any discrepancies between the physical and book inventories must be investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately. Require the Accounting Unit to reconcile immediately the PPE for any discrepancies between the physical and book balances and adjust the noted difference and duly support any adjustments/corrections resulting from the reconciliation with proper documentation. Likewise, the Property and Accounting Units shall conduct periodic reconciliation of their records to ensure that the properties are properly recorded; and

Require the GSO and concerned personnel to exert more effort in securing the necessary documents to establish the ownership of the subject lots.
